item.title: 1.5.1 beta
item.description:
1.5.1

  • Adjustments of all tables
    • The last column is now fixed to the end of the table
    • If the last column has been previously adjusted, it'll might not be at the end of the table.
      => In this case reduce the window width so the column reaches the end of the table.
  • Wrapping positions!

item.title: 1.5.2 beta
item.description:
1.5.2

  • Fix alignments of textfields in the position tab

item.title: 1.7.0 beta
item.description:
1.7.0

  • Playlist overhaul
  • Loop rework
  • Pre-play countdown
  • Clock window remembers open state and is now resizable with a fixed aspect ratio and auto-scaling font
  • Beats countdown before a position starts
  • PDF layout settings and preview for positions
  • Mono/proportional font selection for the position table
  • Replaced id3lib and BASS/BASS FX with TagLib and native BPM detection
  • Faster Song load times

item.title: 1.7.1 beta
item.description:
1.7.1

  • Audio Compression
    A dynamic compressor can now be enabled in Settings > General to reduce the volume difference between loud and quiet passages during playback.

    How to enable: Go to Settings > General and check "Enable Compression". Then reload the currently loaded song for the change to take effect.

    Parameters:
    • Threshold (dB): The volume level above which compression kicks in. A lower value (e.g. -24 dB) means more of the audio gets compressed. Default: -18 dB.
    • Head Room (dB): Controls how gently the compression is applied. A higher value results in softer, more gradual compression. Default: 5 dB.
    • Master Gain (dB): Makeup gain to restore overall volume after compression has reduced the louder parts. Increase this if the song sounds too quiet after compression. Default: 0 dB.

    Click the info button (?) next to the compression settings for a quick reference of these parameters.

  • Improved Audio Normalization for stereo files
    The normalization algorithm now correctly analyzes both channels of stereo files and uses the peak of the louder channel to calculate the gain adjustment, preventing clipping on either side.

  • Clock Window improvements
    The title bar and traffic light buttons have been removed for a cleaner look. Additionally, the clock window can now become fully transparent when it is not being hovered with the mouse. Enable this in Settings > General with the "Transparent when inactive" option. When you move the mouse over the clock window it returns to its normal appearance; move the mouse away and it fades back to transparent.

item.title: 1.7.2 beta
item.description:
1.7.2

  • Position Timeline
    The seek slider in the player has been replaced by a visual timeline bar that shows all dance positions at their exact time positions. A blue fill indicates playback progress, a white playhead line marks the current position, and each named position appears as a tick mark with its label below the bar. You can click or drag anywhere on the bar to seek.

    The timeline is enabled by default. To switch back to the plain slider, go to Settings > General and uncheck "Show Timeline".

  • Customizable Keyboard Shortcuts
    All keyboard shortcuts can now be configured freely in Settings > Shortcuts. Shortcuts are organised into sections (File, Edit, Tools, Player, and Others) and each action shows its current binding. Click a binding field and press the desired key combination to reassign it; click it again while it is active to clear the shortcut entirely.

    Notable shortcuts in the "Others" section:
    • Jump to Position 1–10: Press the corresponding digit key (1–0 on the number row) to jump directly to that position.
    • Jump to Position 11–20: Hold Shift while pressing the digit key.
    • Play Position / Load Song: Press Enter to play the selected position or load the selected song.

    Use the "Reset to Defaults" button at the bottom of the Shortcuts settings page to restore all shortcuts to their factory values.
